contraindications.contraindication_name: respiratory depression / acute or severe asthma
contraindications.reason: Opioids can cause dose-dependent respiratory depression, may exacerbate bronchoconstriction, and should not be used in acute asthma or status asthmaticus.
Opioids reduce gastrointestinal motility and may cause or worsen ileus.
Opioids increase ICP, may interfere with neurological assessment, and risk worsening outcomes.
Known hypersensitivity to opium, morphine, or related alkaloids can trigger severe allergic reactions including anaphylaxis.

contraindications.contraindication_name: concurrent or recent MAO inhibitor use
contraindications.reason: Combination with MAOIs or use within 14 days of MAOI discontinuation may provoke serotonin syndrome, hyperpyrexia, severe respiratory depression, or hemodynamic instability.
Impaired metabolism of opium alkaloids increases risk of accumulation, excessive sedation, and precipitating encephalopathy.
Pregnancy Category C; chronic use may cause neonatal opioid withdrawal syndrome; use only if benefit outweighs risk and for short periods.
Opium alkaloids are excreted in breast milk; may cause sedation and respiratory depression in infants; avoid prolonged use.
Opioids may increase bladder sphincter tone and precipitate acute urinary retention.
Opioid-induced histamine release and vasodilation may worsen hypotension.
Known hypersensitivity to crofelemer or any excipient can lead to allergic reactions.
Crofelemer is indicated for non-infectious diarrhea; using it in bacterial or parasitic diarrhea may mask symptoms and delay appropriate anti-infective treatment.
Pregnancy Category C; minimal systemic absorption expected, but limited human data; use only if potential benefit justifies potential fetal risk.
Safety and efficacy not established in children; use only under careful risk-benefit assessment.

generic_name: Electrolyte Oral Solution, Freezer-Pops, and Jell Cups

contraindications.reason: Inability to excrete electrolytes may lead to life-threatening hyperkalemia, hypernatremia, and fluid overload.
Oral administration may worsen obstruction or result in leakage, infection, and metabolic complications.
These solutions contain glucose; patients with glucose-galactose malabsorption cannot absorb them, leading to severe diarrhea and dehydration.
Allergic reactions may occur with any component (e.g., flavorings, dyes, electrolytes).

contraindications.contraindication_name: severe vomiting / inability to tolerate oral intake
contraindications.reason: If vomiting is intractable, oral rehydration will fail and intravenous therapy should be used.
Solutions typically contain potassium and sodium; use cautiously in conditions where electrolyte restriction is required (e.g., CKD, mineralocorticoid excess).
Pregnancy Category C varies by formulation; generally considered low risk but should be used when clearly needed under medical supervision.
Electrolytes cannot be excreted, causing dangerous hyperkalemia, hypernatremia, and volume overload.
Oral fluids in the setting of obstruction may precipitate perforation or metabolic disturbances.
Contains carbohydrates; patients with this absorption defect will develop profound diarrhea and dehydration.
Reactions to sweeteners, flavors, or preservatives may occur.
When oral intake cannot be retained, intravenous rehydration is indicated.
Heart failure, severe renal impairment, and hyperkalemia/hypernatremia may be worsened by standard electrolyte solutions.
Pregnancy Category C for some formulations; generally compatible, but use under advice in hyperemesis gravidarum situations.
Potassium and sodium retention may rapidly result in cardiotoxicity and fluid overload.
Exogenous electrolyte load can provoke life-threatening arrhythmias or encephalopathy.
Tablets could lodge and cause local injury; absorption is also unreliable.
Risk of allergic or anaphylactic reactions.

contraindications.contraindication_name: severe dysphagia / inability to swallow tablets
contraindications.reason: Alternative formulations (solution, intravenous) should be used to maintain hydration and electrolyte balance.

contraindications.contraindication_name: chronic kidney disease with impaired K+ excretion
contraindications.reason: Even mild to moderate renal impairment may restrict potassium handling; monitor serum K+ closely.
Pregnancy Category C; use only when clearly necessary and with electrolyte monitoring.

contraindications.contraindication_name: known hypersensitivity to difenoxin, atropine, or belladonna alkaloids
contraindications.reason: Allergic reactions ranging from rash to anaphylaxis may occur.
Atropine has anticholinergic properties that can delay biliary excretion and worsen jaundice.
Atropine-induced mydriasis can precipitate acute angle-closure attack and vision loss.
Anticholinergic effect of atropine aggravates bladder outlet obstruction and may cause acute urinary retention.
Reduced gut motility may promote toxin retention and worsen severity of colitis, increasing risk of toxic megacolon.
Difenoxin metabolism is hepatic; accumulation can lead to excessive opioid and anticholinergic toxicity.
Infants and young children are particularly sensitive to opioid-induced respiratory depression and anticholinergic effects; deaths reported.
Pregnancy Category C; may cause fetal harm; use only if clearly needed and for shortest duration.
Atropine can unmask or exacerbate muscle weakness.

contraindications.contraindication_name: untreated bacterial skin infection (e.g., impetigo, cellulitis)
contraindications.reason: Topical corticosteroids suppress local immune response and may mask or worsen bacterial proliferation.

contraindications.contraindication_name: untreated fungal skin infection (e.g., dermatophytosis, candidiasis)
contraindications.reason: Corticosteroid-induced immunosuppression can exacerbate fungal overgrowth.

contraindications.contraindication_name: untreated viral skin infection (e.g., herpes simplex, varicella zoster, vaccinia)
contraindications.reason: May lead to disseminated viral infection and more severe clinical course.
Steroids can exacerbate or induce steroid-induced rosacea and worsen telangiectasias.
May precipitate or aggravate perioral dermatitis, and withdrawal can cause severe rebound flares.
Immediate-type or contact dermatitis can occur.

contraindications.contraindication_name: application on large body surface area or under occlusive dressings (prolonged)
contraindications.reason: Increases systemic absorption, leading to HPA axis suppression and growth retardation in children.

contraindications.contraindication_name: pediatric patients requiring long-term or extensive use
contraindications.reason: Children are at higher risk for systemic toxicity, growth suppression, and intracranial hypertension.
Pregnancy Category C; avoid extensive use or occlusive dressings; may be associated with fetal growth restriction in high cumulative doses.

contraindications.contraindication_name: untreated bacterial skin infection (e.g., impetigo, cellulitis)
contraindications.reason: Local immunosuppression can promote bacterial expansion and mask clinical signs.

contraindications.contraindication_name: untreated fungal skin infection (e.g., dermatophytosis, candidiasis)
contraindications.reason: Steroids encourage fungal growth and may complicate management.

contraindications.contraindication_name: untreated viral skin infection (e.g., herpes simplex, varicella zoster, vaccinia)
contraindications.reason: Risk of dissemination and more severe or atypical viral presentations.
May induce steroid rosacea and significant rebound upon withdrawal.
Known to provoke or aggravate perioral dermatitis.
Allergic contact dermatitis and immediate reactions are possible.

contraindications.contraindication_name: application on face, groin, or axillae (high-potency formulations)
contraindications.reason: Thin skin in these regions increases systemic absorption and risk of atrophy, striae, and telangiectasia.

contraindications.contraindication_name: extensive or prolonged use in children
contraindications.reason: Higher risk of HPA axis suppression, growth retardation, and Cushing's syndrome.
Pregnancy Category C; avoid large amounts or occlusive dressings; systemic exposure may pose risk to fetus.

contraindications.contraindication_name: hypersensitivity to dupilumab or any excipient
contraindications.reason: May cause severe allergic reactions including anaphylaxis and serum sickness-like illness.

contraindications.contraindication_name: acute asthma symptoms or status asthmaticus
contraindications.reason: Dupilumab is not a rescue bronchodilator; initiate in stable patients only.
Eosinophil-modulating therapy may reduce host defenses; treat helminth infections before initiating dupilumab.
Immunomodulation may increase risk of vaccine-related infection; complete age-appropriate live immunizations before therapy.
Pregnancy Category N; no well-controlled human data; use only if potential benefit justifies unknown fetal risk.
Unknown if excreted in human milk; caution advised, especially in preterm or immunocompromised infants.

contraindications.contraindication_name: untreated local bacterial, fungal, or viral infection

contraindications.contraindication_name: hypersensitivity to hydrocortisone or suppository base
contraindications.reason: Contact hypersensitivity reactions may worsen local symptoms.
Pregnancy Category C; systemic absorption possible, particularly with inflamed mucosa; use only if clearly needed.
Pregnancy Category X; known teratogen causing severe fetal malformations and death; must be avoided in pregnancy or if pregnancy planned.
Excreted in breast milk in clinically significant amounts; contraindicated due to risk of infant immunosuppression and carcinogenicity.

contraindications.contraindication_name: severe hepatic impairment / active liver disease
contraindications.reason: Hepatotoxic agent; baseline liver disease increases risk of cirrhosis and fulminant hepatitis.
Renal excretion is primary; accumulation leads to severe bone marrow suppression and mucositis.

contraindications.contraindication_name: preexisting bone marrow suppression / significant cytopenias
contraindications.reason: Profound myelosuppression can result in life-threatening infection and bleeding.
Further immunosuppression may produce overwhelming sepsis.
Synergistic hepatotoxicity increases risk of cirrhosis and liver failure.
Allergic pneumonitis, rash, and anaphylaxis have been reported.

Methotrexate distributes into third spaces, delaying clearance and increasing toxicity; drain effusions before therapy.
May exacerbate mucosal injury and precipitate gastrointestinal bleeding.
Superpotent immunosuppression promotes bacterial growth and masks infection.
